title: "X11 conf file location"
date: 2018-12-08
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by bradhaack on 2018-12-08
I'm changing 70-synaptics.conf to work better for my macbook.    Where do I put it so it won't be lost when I get a distro update?    I found the file in  /usr/share/X11/xorg.conf.d .   In the file it says
```
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE, your distribution will likely overwrite
# it when updating. Copy (and rename) this file into
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf.d first
```
However, I do not have an xorg.conf.d directory in /etc/X11.    Is the note correct?   Should I create this directory?  What should I rename the file to?

---

### Post by CatKiller on 2018-12-08
The files in /usr/share/X11/ are just example files. Files in /etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/ will be used.

You can create the directory. I believe the numbers are to do with the order they are processed in. It's to make it easier to manage than when everything was put in a single xorg.conf file.

---
